Class ResourceController

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.controller.resource.ResourceController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1") public class ResourceController extends Object

  • Constructor Details

    • ResourceController

      public ResourceController()

  • Method Details

    • getResourcesByResourceGroup

      @GetMapping("/resourcegroups/{groupId}/resources") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Set<Resource>> getResourcesByResourceGroup(@PathVariable Long groupId)
      Returns all resources of a specific resource group.
      Parameters:
      groupId - ID of the resource group
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the set of resources

    • createResourceGroup

      @PostMapping("/departments/{deptId}/resourcegroups") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ResourceGroup> createResourceGroup(@PathVariable Long deptId, @RequestParam String name, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Creates a new resource group for a specific department.
      Parameters:
      deptId - ID of the department
      name - name of the new resource group
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the newly created resource group

    • deleteResourceGroup

      @DeleteMapping("/resourcegroups/{groupId}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> deleteResourceGroup(@PathVariable Long groupId, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Deletes a resource group, if the current user is authorized.
      Parameters:
      groupId - ID of the resource group to delete
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity without body

    • createResource

      @PostMapping("/resourcegroups/{groupId}/resources") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Resource> createResource(@PathVariable Long groupId, @RequestBody Resource resource, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Creates a new resource in a specific resource group.
      Parameters:
      groupId - ID of the resource group
      resource - resource to be created
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the newly created resource

    • getResource

      @GetMapping("/resources/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Resource> getResource(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Retrieves a resource, if the current user is authorized.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the resource

    • partialUpdateResource

      @PatchMapping("/resources/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Resource> partialUpdateResource(@PathVariable Long id, @RequestBody Resource patch, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Updates individual fields of a resource (PATCH semantics: null = unchanged).
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      patch - resource with the fields to be changed
      Returns:
      the updated resource

    • deleteResource

      @DeleteMapping("/resources/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> deleteResource(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Deletes a resource, if the current user is authorized.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource to delete
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity without body

    • reserveResource

      @PostMapping("/resources/{id}/reserve") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<ResourceReservation> reserveResource(@PathVariable Long id, @RequestParam String fromIsoDate, @RequestParam String untilIsoDate, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Reserves a resource for a specific time span.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      fromIsoDate - start date in ISO format (e.g. 2026-05-15T14:00:00Z)
      untilIsoDate - end date in ISO format (e.g. 2026-05-15T16:00:00Z)
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the new reservation

    • getMyActiveReservations

      @GetMapping("/resources/{id}/reservations/mine") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ResourceReservation>> getMyActiveReservations(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Returns the caller's own currently active reservations on this resource. Useful as a preview of which slot a control command derives: empty → command not possible; exactly one → its slot; multiple → command ambiguous.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      Returns:
      list of the caller's own active reservations (possibly empty)

    • getReservationsForResource

      @GetMapping("/resources/{id}/reservations") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<ResourceReservation>> getReservationsForResource(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Returns all reservations of a resource (occupancy overview). Requires read permission on the resource.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      Returns:
      list of all reservations of the resource

    • cancelReservation

      @DeleteMapping("/reservations/{reservationId}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> cancelReservation(@PathVariable Integer reservationId, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Cancels a reservation and releases the occupied slot. Allowed for the owner of the reservation or a user with UPDATE rights on the resource.
      Parameters:
      reservationId - ID of the reservation to cancel
      Returns:
      204 No Content on success

    • sendCommand

      @PostMapping("/resources/{id}/command") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> sendCommand(@PathVariable Long id, @RequestBody ResourceController.ResourceCommandRequest request, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Sends a control command to a resource. The transport (MQTT/REST) is chosen automatically (MQTT preferred when online, otherwise REST fallback). The addressed slot is derived server-side from the user's active reservation — the client supplies no slot.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      request - command and optional free parameter
      Returns:
      202 Accepted, if the command was handed off

    • recordValue

      @PostMapping("/resources/{id}/values") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> recordValue(@PathVariable Long id, @RequestBody ResourceController.ResourceValueRequest request, org.springframework.security.core.Authentication auth)
      Records a telemetry reading for a resource (REST ingest). Enables devices without MQTT — and manual/Bruno calls — to report values. The reading is appended to the named data point's history, mirroring the MQTT VALUE path. Requires UPDATE permission on the resource.
      Parameters:
      id - ID of the resource
      request - data point name and value (both mandatory)
      Returns:
      202 Accepted once the reading has been recorded

    • getSkillsForResource

      @GetMapping("/resources/{resourceId}/skills") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Set<SkillRecord>> getSkillsForResource(@PathVariable Long resourceId)
      Returns all skills of a resource.
      Parameters:
      resourceId - ID of the resource
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the set of SkillRecords

    • getSkillsForResourceInGroup

      @GetMapping("/resourcegroups/{groupId}/resources/{resourceId}/skills") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Set<SkillRecord>> getSkillsForResourceInGroup(@PathVariable Long groupId, @PathVariable Long resourceId)
      Returns all skills of a resource, filtered by resource group. Additionally validates whether the resource belongs to the given group.
      Parameters:
      groupId - ID of the resource group
      resourceId - ID of the resource
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the set of SkillRecords

    • assignSkillToResource

      @PostMapping("/resources/{resourceId}/skills") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<SkillRecord> assignSkillToResource(@PathVariable Long resourceId, @RequestBody SkillRecord record)
      Assigns a skill to a resource.
      Parameters:
      resourceId - ID of the resource
      record - The SkillRecord to assign
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity with the created SkillRecord
